methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Teramount is an acquired-stage company in Chips & Semiconductors founded by Hesham Taha and Avi Israel, having raised $50M+. The relevant comp universe consists of 3 public semiconductor peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private companies in the sector, including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

Two standard valuation triangulation methods were evaluated. Peer-Median analysis proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow peer depth: while 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, filtering by same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints in each cohort, falling short of the minimum threshold for statistical reliability. Public-Comp Anchoring, which would leverage the Semiconductors sector P/S median of 25.3x across the three public comparables, could not be applied because Teramount's revenue was not available on file—a common constraint with private companies.

The data constraints prevent a quantitative triangulation at this time. Meaningful valuation synthesis would require either disclosed revenue figures for the target or identification of 3+ statistically comparable private peers at an equivalent stage and scale. Given Teramount's acquisition status, a transaction valuation would supersede multiples-based analysis entirely. Key uncertainties center on the availability of transaction economics and the degree to which the broader private chip-design peer set (Cerebras, Etched, SambaNova) reflects comparable risk and growth profiles.
